text: Running-related injury incidence per 1000 hours of running ranges from 2.5 in long-distance track and field athletes to 33.0 in novice runners.
Novice runners have a weighted injury incidence of 17.8 per 1000 hours of running.
Recreational runners have a weighted injury incidence of 7.7 per 1000 hours of running.
Novice runners face approximately 2.3 times greater injury risk per 1000 hours of running compared to recreational runners.
Long-distance track and field athletes have the lowest reported injury incidence at 2.5 per 1000 hours of running, suggesting experienced and structured training reduces injury risk.
